introduction: Today, we'll be taking a look at our IT Support's performance in January 2025. This will provide us with insights into our operations and potential areas for improvement.

subsections.heading: IT Support Ticket Categories Breakdown
subsections.content:
• Technical issues made up 60% of all support tickets in 2025
• 20% of tickets were related to software updates
• Login and authentication issues accounted for 10% of all tickets
• 7% of the tickets were related to hardware failures
• The remaining 3% were miscellaneous issues, including connectivity problems and user errors
• The majority of technical issues were related to the use of our telehealth platform
• Software update issues were mostly due to compatibility problems with different devices
subsections.justification: While this provides a breakdown of ticket categories, it does not provide the total number of tickets processed. This information is tangentially related.

subsections.heading: Volume of IT Support Tickets in January 2025
subsections.content:
• There was a 15% increase in the number of tickets compared to December 2024
• The first week of January saw the highest volume of tickets
• 40% of tickets were resolved within the first 24 hours
• The highest volume of tickets came from the province of Ontario
• There was a significant drop in ticket volume during the last week of January
• Approximately 20% of tickets remained unresolved by the end of January
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the volume of tickets in different aspects but doesn't provide the total number of tickets processed in January 2025. This information is tangentially related.

subsections.heading: Resolution Time for IT Support Tickets
subsections.content:
• The average resolution time was 48 hours in January 2025
• 30% of tickets took more than 72 hours to resolve
• Tickets related to technical issues took the longest to resolve, with an average of 72 hours
• Tickets related to login issues had the shortest resolution time, with an average of 24 hours
• The IT department implemented a new ticketing system mid-January, which reduced resolution times by 10%
• There was a backlog of unresolved tickets from December 2024 that were resolved in January 2025
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the resolution time of tickets but doesn't provide the total number of tickets processed in January 2025. This information is tangentially related.

conclusion: In conclusion, our IT support performed optimally in January with an acceptable number of tickets. Moving forward, we will focus on areas that require improvement to ensure seamless operations.
